medical_history.history_of_present_illness: PMH of glaucoma, diverticulitis, dementia presented to ___ with left lower quadrant abdominal unclear duration and SOB. Per niece he had it through the evening. No vomiting. Non-exertional. Patient has dementia at baseline and unable to provide further history. At ___, He had a HR of 99 and BP of 136/100. Exam was notable for heme-negative stool and left lower quadrant tenderness. EKG showed sinus 94 with Normal axis. STE leads 3 and aVF 3-4 mm and lead 2 elevations of 2 mm. ST depressions 1, aVL, V2/3 greater than 2 mm. He received ASA, heparin and plavix load at ___ and ___ to ___ for catheterization. At the cath lab in ___, he was found to have 99% RCA mid stenosis with thrombus, and Focal 80% before D1 and 80-90% tubular after D1 LAD lesion. He had DES placed in RCA.
medical_history.family_history: Father had an MI in his late ___. Otherwise no family history of early MI, arrhythmia, cardiomyopathies, or sudden cardiac death; otherwise non-contributory.

physical_examination.general_examination: VS: T= 97.9 BP=103/70 HR=90 RR= 23 O2 sat= 98% on 2L. General: Pleasant, fidgety, able to communicate short sentences, but some confabulating.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Unable to cooperate fully with exam. Cardiovascular: Normal S1, S2, non-displaced PMI, no murmurs.
